banner.text: EU & British residents: You can vote in 2027 but must register first.
hero.title: Your Town. Your Vote. Your Future.
hero.subtitle: Citizens running for their town — not politicians running for power. 47% of Sitges residents have never had a unified voice. United for Sitges changes that.
hero.stat1.label: Registered Residents
hero.stat2.label: Born outside Catalonia
hero.stat3.label: Votes Won 2023 Election
hero.stat4.label: Votes Needed for Majority
counter.eu.label: EU citizens not yet registered to vote
counter.spanish.label: Spanish citizens from other regions
counter.latam.label: Latin Americans with potential voting rights
counter.seats.label: Council seats — 11 needed for majority
movement.title: A dormant majority. Finally awake.
movement.body1: Sitges has a dormant majority that has never been activated as a bloc. The political landscape is fragmented — 9 parties sharing 21 seats, with the winner in 2023 needing just 1,875 votes. This creates an extraordinary opportunity.
movement.quote: “Sitges belongs to everyone who calls it home. The 47% have a voice. It is time to use it.”
movement.card.body: ~15,200 residents from across the world. Scattered across parties or completely disengaged. United for Sitges is the first movement built for all of them.
positioning.title: What we are — and what we are not.
positioning.row5.is: Multilingual by default. All languages respected.
truths.title: Home. Voice. Future.
truths.1.title: Your home.
truths.1.body: Sitges is your town whether you were born here or chose to live here. That choice matters.
truths.2.title: Your voice.
truths.2.body: 47% of residents have never been spoken to as a community. We change that.
truths.3.title: Your future.
truths.3.body: Practical governance. A stable majority. A Sitges that works for everyone.
audiences.title: One movement, many origins.
audiences.lede: The brand speaks to every community at a B2 language level — warm, direct, and never leaving anyone feeling like an afterthought.
audiences.british.desc: Long-established homeowners, 45+. Many unaware they regained voting rights after Brexit via the 2019 UK–Spain bilateral agreement.
audiences.spanish.desc: From Andalucía, Madrid, Valencia. Already on the electoral roll. Feel like guests in their own country — no party has ever spoken to them in Sitges.
audiences.spanish.feel: Finally, someone built this for me too.
audiences.italian.desc: The largest votable EU group. Strong community identity, all ages. High potential if reached via the Italian residents' network.
audiences.french.desc: Politically engaged at home, disengaged in Spain. Educated, confident. Will respond to a clear civic argument delivered in French.
audiences.latam.desc: Colombian, Bolivian, Peruvian, Brazilian, Venezuelan — community-oriented, warm, family-focused. Voting rights via reciprocity after 5 years residency.
audiences.german.desc: Practical, efficiency-focused. Drawn to the good-governance argument above all others. High home-ownership rates — the housing platform resonates strongly.
audiences.german.feel: This is how we fix things.
numbers.title: Why 2027. Why now. Why you.
numbers.lede: The 2023 election was won with just 1,875 votes out of 11,092 cast. Nine parties split 21 seats. The maths of winning in 2027 are straightforward — if the dormant majority registers and votes.
numbers.table1.heading: 2023 Sitges Municipal Election Results
numbers.junts.note: Opposition — won most votes but not in government
numbers.comuns.note: Coalition partner — taking over mayorship Jun 2026
numbers.total.label: TOTAL VOTES CAST
numbers.insight1.label: votes won the most seats in 2023
numbers.insight1.note: Out of 11,092 cast. That's just 16.9% of the vote winning the lead position. Fragmentation is our opportunity.
numbers.insight2.label: votes needed for an absolute majority
numbers.insight2.note: 11 of 21 seats. At 53% turnout this requires ~5,000–5,500 votes. The unregistered EU + British + Latin American pool alone exceeds this.
numbers.insight3.label: eligible EU voters not yet on the register
numbers.insight3.note: Historical registration rate among EU expats in Spanish coastal towns: 10–20%. This gap is the single greatest untapped opportunity in Sitges politics.
numbers.table2.heading: Who Lives in Sitges — and Who Can Vote
numbers.table2.source: Source: Idescat 2025 / INE padrón. Voter eligibility based on Spanish electoral law (LOREG) and bilateral agreements.
numbers.row.spanish.name: Spanish citizens from other regions
numbers.row.eu.name: 🇫🇷🇳🇱🇩🇪🇮🇹🇸🇪 EU citizens
numbers.row.british.name: British residents
numbers.row.british.sub: Largest single foreign group in Sitges
numbers.row.latam.name: Latin Americans (reciprocity)
numbers.total2.label: TOTAL ADDRESSABLE VOTE POOL
numbers.total2.sub: (if all eligible residents registered & voted at 53%)
numbers.total2.note: New voters still to register: 3,000–6,000+ EU + 1,105 British + ~849 Latin American
numbers.footnote: * Potential votes estimated at 53% turnout (2023 rate). EU figure assumes 30% registration uptake from unregistered pool.
supporters.title: This is how we win — and why it matters
supporters.lede: Sitges has had nine parties sharing 21 seats for years. The result is paralysis — slow permits, no housing policy, seasonal governance, and no multilingual services for the 47% of residents who aren't Catalan-born. United for Sitges is the first movement built specifically to change this.
supporters.why.heading: Why hasn't this happened before?
supporters.reason1.title: All existing parties are Catalan nationalist or regional
supporters.reason1.body: Their voter base is Catalan-born. None have incentive to mobilise the 47%.
supporters.reason2.title: EU registration requires proactive outreach
supporters.reason2.body: EU citizens must apply separately to the CERE — they are not automatically added. No party has ever run a CERE drive in Sitges.
supporters.reason3.title: The language barrier is a deliberate feature
supporters.reason3.body: All campaigns run exclusively in Catalan. The 47% are effectively invisible to the political system.
supporters.reason4.title: The leadership instability creates the opening
supporters.reason4.body: The current minority government (ERC + GI + Comuns + FETS, 10/21 seats) is handing power to Comuns Verds in June 2026, creating visible instability right as United for Sitges launches.
supporters.win.heading: What winning looks like
supporters.scenario1.name: Outright majority
supporters.scenario1.outcome: Govern alone. Full 4-year mandate to implement all six platform pillars.
supporters.scenario2.name: Largest party
supporters.scenario2.outcome: Lead coalition with PSC or Comuns Verds. Negotiate key platform commitments as coalition terms.
supporters.scenario3.name: Significant bloc
supporters.scenario3.outcome: Constructive opposition. Force multilingual services and housing agenda. Build credibility for 2031.
supporters.practice.heading: What your support means in practice
supporters.action1.title: Register yourself
supporters.action1.body: If you're an EU citizen or British resident not yet on the electoral register, this is the single highest-impact action you can take.
supporters.action2.title: Tell three neighbours
supporters.action2.body: The most effective political communication is peer-to-peer. Word-of-mouth in the Sitges Expats Club, WhatsApp groups, and school gates can reach thousands.
supporters.action3.title: Volunteer two hours
supporters.action3.body: Saturday morning at the Mercat Municipal with a registration stand. Two hours, 10+ new registered voters. That's the maths.
supporters.action4.title: Fund the infrastructure
supporters.action4.body: The voter registration operation alone costs €8,000–15,000. Printing, events, digital — every donation goes directly into mobilising new voters.
platform.title: Six pillars for a Sitges that works
platform.lede: Practical governance, multilingual services, affordable housing, sustainable tourism, safety, and community integration.
platform.p1.title: Practical, Efficient Local Government
platform.p1.li3: Public dashboard showing spending in real time
platform.p2.title: Multilingual Municipal Services
platform.p2.li1: Catalan, Spanish & English as minimum in all key comms
platform.p2.li2: English-language section of the Ajuntament website
platform.p2.li3: Multilingual info sessions for new residents
platform.p3.title: Housing & Quality of Life
platform.p4.title: Sustainable Tourism Management
platform.p4.li1: Tourism must serve residents, not overwhelm them
platform.p4.li2: Invest tourist tax revenue in resident infrastructure
platform.p4.li3: Traffic & parking solutions during peak season
platform.p5.title: Safe, Clean, Green Sitges
platform.p5.li3: Expand cycling infrastructure; reduce car dependency
platform.p6.title: Community Integration
platform.p6.li1: Welcome programmes for new residents of all origins
platform.p6.li2: Annual Residents Congress — all communities represented
platform.p6.li3: Celebrate Sitges's diversity as its competitive advantage
votingRights.title: Who can vote in Sitges?
votingRights.subtitle: Not all residents have the same voting rights in Spanish municipal elections. Check your status below — then take action.
votingRights.row1.name: Spanish citizens from other regions
votingRights.row2.name: EU Citizens
votingRights.row3.name: Latin Americans (reciprocity)
votingRights.row4.name: 🇬🇧 British residents
votingRights.row4.sub: 3+ years legal residency required. Largest single foreign group in Sitges (1,105 residents).
votingRights.row5.name: Other non-EU (Moroccan, Argentine, Mexican…)
cere.title: EU Citizens: Register on the CERE
cere.body: The CERE (Censo Electoral de Residentes Extranjeros Comunitarios) is managed by the INE. Only 10–20% of eligible EU expats in Spanish coastal towns are registered. Registering 3,000 new voters alone can deliver an absolute majority — and no party has ever tried this before.
cere.step1.title: Get your documents
cere.step1.body: Valid passport or EU ID card + proof of Sitges padrón (empadronamiento) registration
cere.step2.title: Fill in Modelo 1
cere.step2.body: Available at the INE office or the Sitges Ajuntament. Simple one-page form — takes 10 minutes
cere.step3.title: Stay registered
cere.step3.body: Once on the CERE, you remain registered for all future municipal elections. You only need to do this once.
uk.title: British Residents: You CAN vote — via the 2019 UK–Spain Bilateral Agreement
uk.body: Despite Brexit, British residents retained municipal voting rights in Spain through a bilateral agreement signed in 2019. You need 3 years of legal residency in Spain. You are the single largest foreign national group in Sitges — 1,105 residents — and your votes can be decisive.
uk.step1.title: Check eligibility
uk.step1.body: British passport + 3 years continuous legal residency in Spain + empadronamiento in Sitges
uk.step2.title: Submit your application
uk.step2.body: Apply to the INE using the UK bilateral registration form. Our team can guide you through the process — register below.
uk.step3.title: Check the deadline
uk.step3.body: Registration deadlines are strict. Contact us now — do not wait until 2027. Every registered voter matters.
timeline.title: From movement to majority
timeline.lede: The 2027 municipal elections are the target. Every month matters. Here is the roadmap.
timeline.phase0.title: Phase 0: Foundation
timeline.phase0.body: Agrupación de electores setup, candidate selection, core team formation, legal setup, HQ established
timeline.phase1.title: Phase 1: Voter Registration Drive
timeline.phase1.body: CERE + reciprocity registration campaign, community outreach, database building, expat partnerships
timeline.phase2.title: Phase 2: Platform Launch
timeline.phase2.body: Full platform in 4 languages, media campaign, community forums, summer beach presence
timeline.phase3.title: Phase 3: Full Campaign
timeline.phase3.body: Door-to-door canvassing, rallies, debates, get-out-the-vote (GOTV), polling station coverage
timeline.election.title: Election Day
timeline.election.body: Full mobilisation. The 47% vote. A new Sitges begins.
join.title: Register your support
join.subtitle: Add your name. Spread the word. Help us reach the 47% — and win.
join.reason1.title: CERE registration assistance
join.reason1.body: We'll guide EU citizens through the CERE registration process step by step.
join.reason2.title: Campaign updates in your language
join.reason2.body: News, events, and registration reminders in English, Spanish, Italian, French, Dutch, or German.
join.reason3.title: Volunteer opportunities
join.reason3.body: Registration stands, canvassing, events — make a real difference in your neighbourhood.
join.reason4.title: Founding member status
join.reason4.body: Be part of history. Be one of the first 500 supporters of United for Sitges.
join.form.heading: Register Your Support
join.form.sub: Takes 60 seconds. Free. No spam, ever.
join.form.newsletter: Send me campaign updates and voter registration reminders
join.success.title: You're in. Thank you!
join.success.body: We'll be in touch with your voter registration guide and campaign updates. Welcome to the movement.
donate.title: Fund the movement
donate.body: Running a political campaign takes resources. The total campaign budget is €35,000–70,000 — funded entirely by residents like you. Every euro goes directly into voter registration, community outreach, and communications.
donate.li2: Community events, forums, and outreach stands
donate.li4: Campaign HQ and full-time campaign team
donate.legal: Note: Spanish electoral law (LOREG) limits and governs campaign financing. Full transparency disclosure will be published prior to the official campaign period.
donate.form.heading: Make a donation
donate.subtitle: Secure payment. Any amount helps.
donate.form.name: Full name
donate.secure: Secure & encrypted. Donations subject to Spanish electoral law.
donate.success.title: Thank you for your support!
donate.success.body: You'll receive a confirmation email. Your donation powers the movement.
footer.movement.heading: Movement
footer.action.heading: Take Action
footer.description: A pro-Sitges movement for the 2027 municipal elections. Built by and for every resident who calls Sitges home.
footer.tagline: Your Town. Your Vote. Your Future.
footer.copyright: © 2026 United for Sitges. All rights reserved. Subject to Spanish electoral law.
